instruction: Design a birthday party plan for a 5-year-old, specifying games, snacks, and decorations. The plan must include at least three games, with one being a team-based activity. The letter "S" must appear exactly 15 times throughout the response. The entire response must be written in English and include a relevant English idiom. Maintain a cheerful and positive tone throughout the response. The phrase "fun for everyone" must appear exactly twice. The response must contain exactly 10 sentences, with each paragraph containing at least 3 sentences. The keyword "fun" must not appear in the response.
response:
Start with bright balloons and starry streamers to set the scene.
This setup ensures fun for everyone at the celebration.
We will play a simple game called Simon Says for all kids.

Next, divide the group into two teams for a sack race.
Team spirit makes the activity exciting for every child present.
This team-based game brings smiles to every single face.

Serve sweet strawberries and soft sandwiches as tasty treats.
The snacks are chosen to suit small appetites perfectly.
Such food choices keep energy high throughout the day.

Another activity involves sorting shiny stones by color.
Children love finding patterns while they play outside.
This quiet game offers fun for everyone in the yard.

Finally, end the party with a special surprise song.
Everyone sings together to make memories that last forever.
The whole event feels like a dream come true.

Remember that a little planning goes a long way.
With care, the day becomes truly special for all.
Let the laughter and joy fill the air completely.

Conflicts between Keyword Constraints an.new_constraint: The keyword "fun" must not appear in the response.
Conflicts between Keyword Constraints an.org_constraint: The phrase "fun for everyone" must appear exactly twice.
